items.title: Syria - The rise and fall of the Assad regime | DW Documentary
items.author: DW Documentary
items.content:
<article>On 8 December 8 2024, Syria's dictator Bashar al-Assad fled to Russia with his family. The film, which was shot before and after the fall of the regime, shows why the dictatorship collapsed so quickly and what hopes Syrians have today.

Bashar al-Assad came to power in 2000, after the death of his father. Together with his western-influenced wife Asma, the couple seemed to embody a new beginning for the country. But reforms failed to materialize and when the population took to the streets during the Arab Spring, Bashar al-Assad quickly abandoned his progressive rhetoric. Peaceful demonstrations were met with bloody repression and poison gas attacks. Over 500,000 people were killed, more than 100,000 disappeared and at least six million Syrians were forced into exile.

Among them was the activist Bassam Ezzedine. In 2013, he documented the regime's poison gas attack on the Ghouta region. As a result, he had to flee with his family. His sister and her family remained in Syria and were cruelly punished. She was arrested and her children were taken away from her. 

At the time of the first interview, Bassam, like the rest of the world, cannot know that the regime will soon fall and he will be able to return home. As the filming progresses, change comes at a fast pace.

The war waged by Bashar al-Assad against his own people has destroyed the country’s economic infrastructure. A few weeks before the fall of the regime, Syrian journalist Faysal risked his life to secretly film images of everyday life in Syria: starving children searching for food in the garbage, and endless queues outside bakeries. The country had been bled dry after 54 years of Assad rule. After months of preparation, the Syrian rebels launched a new offensive on 27 November 2024. Within 11 days, they ended the dictatorship - without encountering any serious resistance.

The new government promised an inclusive administration for all. But as Marie Forestier, Syria expert at the European Institute for Peace, explains: "The real challenge for the new president is to broaden his base. Because so far he has relied exclusively on loyalists from Idlib, who are very conservative." And even after the overwhelming victory, the new government does not control the entire territory: in the south, the Israeli army has extended its occupation to the Golan Heights, while in the north, the Turks are bombing Kurdish-controlled areas on a massive scale.

Syria is still unstable. However, the hopes and expectations of Syrians following more than 50 years of Assad rule are high.

items.title: Conquest of a New World | Conquistadors: The Rise and Fall | MEGA EPISODE | National Geographic
items.author: National Geographic
items.content:
<article>In this season one Conquistadors: The Rise and Fall mega episode, National Geographic uncovers the history and legends of infamous conquistadors and their conquests. Featuring Full Episodes: 

A New World
The accidental discovery of the New World by Christopher Columbus sparks an age of exploration for the Spanish empire, and a dark new chapter in the history of the Americas. 

The Caribbean Conquest
Columbus’ return voyage to the Americas begins with bloodshed and a chain of brutal colonization throughout the Caribbean begins. 

The Aztec Conquest
The ruthlessly ambitious Hernán Cortés travels deep into the Mexican interior to conquer one of the great empires in the Americas: the Aztecs.  

The Inca Conquest
Following the success of Hernán Cortés’ over the Aztecs, conquistador Francisco Pizarro sets his sights on conquering the largest civilization in the Americas: the Incas. 

The Growth of the Colonies
After decades of conquest, Spanish colonies have grown throughout the Americas. The Spanish monarchy struggles to control the guardians of ‘New Spain’ from afar. 

The Black Legend
Disdain and jealousy for Spain's wealth and violent practices in the Americas leads to other European rulers propagating an anti-Spanish narrative. The ‘Black Legend’ is born and heralds the end of the era of Conquistadors.

items.author: The People Profiles
items.content:
<article>Dive into the captivating life of Queen Mary of Teck, a pivotal figure in British royal history. This video explores the incredible journey of Mary of Teck, from her early life as Princess Victoria Mary of Teck to her reign as Queen consort to King George V. Discover how Queen Mary navigated the complexities of royal life, including the dramatic changes brought by World War I and the abdication crisis of her son, King Edward VIII.

Learn about her profound influence on the monarchy, her legendary collection of royal jewels, and her unwavering dedication to duty. We uncover the woman behind the stoic public persona, exploring her personal relationships and her enduring legacy. Whether you're a history enthusiast, a fan of the royal family, or simply curious about the life of a queen, this video on Mary of Teck offers a unique and insightful look into a fascinating era of British history. items.title: Our immune system - Miracle weapon against cancer?| DW Documentary
items.author: DW Documentary
items.content:
<article>Our immune system protects us from pathogens, harmful substances and malignant cell changes. For humans, it is indispensable. This film examines the immune system and shows how it can be strengthened - as well as how it can fail.

The immune system does not have a fixed location. Rather, it is comprised of a network of systems distributed throughout the body. It is both complex and fascinating. 
In recent years and especially since COVID, doctors and scientists have collected extensive data on the immune system and gained important new insights. This scientific documentary offers us an absorbing and entertaining look at our immune system, focusing on four key questions: What is the immune system? How can we strengthen it? Under what circumstances does it fail? And how can it be used to cure diseases such as cancer?
The film presents fascinating examples, clear explanations and interviews with international experts. A pediatrician in Hamburg explains how the immune system develops in infants and young children. Immunologists in Paris and New York shed light on the relationship between the microbiome and the immune system. And an oncologist in Heidelberg uses immunotherapy to specifically combat cancer.
Never before has the immune system been the focus of so much scientific research as it is now.

DESCRIPTION
Tim Misiak interviews Sy Brand about their book "Building a Debugger". Sy, who has a background in GPU compilers and debuggers, discusses how implementing a debugger offers profound insights into computer systems.

The conversation explores the unexpected complexities of debugging fundamentals like stack unwinding and code stepping. They examine how debugging interfaces have evolved and the challenges posed by backward compatibility and language evolution.

Sy highlights areas for future advancement in debugging, particularly time travel debugging for non-deterministic issues and better tools for debugging optimized code. Throughout the interview, both experts share fascinating technical details that reveal why debugger implementation serves as an excellent path to deeper understanding of operating systems and hardware.

For aspiring home cooks, the best way to learn is by starting with the essentials and building your confidence. Here's our recommended path:
White Sauces: Begin with Béchamel and its derivatives.
Velouté and Derivatives: Move on to sauces like Allemande and Sauce Suprême.

items.content:
<article>Following arrival in Germany, what’s next? Three people help migrants to build a new life. They advise and translate. They create prospects and encourage people. They live for their commitment - and get a lot back.

Ever since Ukrainian Anhelina Leshak moved to southern Germany, she‘s been helping other refugees. She‘s often the first point of contact, for her compatriots in particular: the 22-year-old helps with job searches and tax forms in Trier, gives new arrivals hope in times of war and supports them as they work to gain a foothold in society. In addition to her voluntary work helping refugees and her full-time job, her next big goal is to represent the interests of migrants in politics.
Bernd Frietsch also has an open ear for the diverse concerns of migrants. From complicated applications to important medical consultations, already tricky situations can become even greater challenges due to language barriers. The 63-year-old even gave up his job as a bassoonist in the orchestra to work in the migration advice service. His willingness to help knows no bounds, even after work. And sometimes clients become good friends.
Entrepreneur Reiner Rudolphi is also dedicated to a project close to his heart: improving the prospects of young people from Rwanda. That‘s why he‘s creating apprenticeships for them in Sembach, in southern Germany. The new skilled workers will not only benefit the German economy, but they will also help industry in Rwanda in the long term. Rudlophi also plans to set up companies there soon to create local jobs. Until then, he aims to show that his trainees are needed - and that they belong.

items.title: How do sinkholes form? - Elise Cutts
items.author: TED-Ed
items.content:
<article>Explore the science of sinkholes, how they commonly form, and what human activities can also trigger them.

--

It was a rainy December morning in San Francisco when the Earth opened up. The ground suddenly fell away into a 12-meter-deep sinkhole that engulfed a garage, part of a street, and a multi-million-dollar mansion. Sinkholes can seem to strike out of nowhere, but they’re more common than people often think. So, how do they form? Elise Cutts details what’s happening just below your feet.

items.title: Maya culture in Belize | DW Documentary
items.author: DW Documentary
items.content:
<article>Many still believe that Maya culture is completely extinct, but in Belize, Maya practices are being actively preserved. Beyond the environmental protection of forests, ancient knowledge is also being kept alive. Maria Garcia’s fight is a part of this struggle. The land is home to medicinal plants and traditional Maya healing practices have been passed down for generations. 

Education and preservation go hand in hand. While Maria protects these sacred lands, young Maya like Frank Tzib are revitalizing and teaching the ancient writing system, ensuring their language, culture, and history are never forgotten. 

Watch the full documentary “The Maya of Belize - The battle for culture, the rainforest and ancient knowledge” on the DW Documentary YouTube channel.
